response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with broad full-stack experience and demonstrable geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map work, which aligns with the routing/navigation context. However, the core technical requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), with no recent systems-level C++ or Rust work. Most recent experience is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The geospatial passion and low-latency DLang background at Sociomantic are transferable signals, but the C++ gap is significant for a Senior SDE role centered on C++ pipelines.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background (Sociomantic, millions of req/s)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, Linux
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust experience; C/C++ work is from 2014-2015
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js, not systems languages
No evidence of distributed-systems work on production routing/data pipelines at the scale described
